title: Climate Change Feedback
definition: The complex, self-reinforcing interactions between global warming and various Earth systems that amplify the effects of climate change.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The interconnected processes by which climate change triggers further changes in Earth's systems, leading to even more warming and severe consequences.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The positive feedback loops between global warming and various factors such as melting ice, deforestation, and altered weather patterns that intensify the rate and severity of climate change.
difficulty_levels.expert: The sophisticated interplay between human-induced climate changes and complex Earth systems, leading to self-reinforcing feedback loops that exacerbate global warming and its impacts on ecosystems, agriculture, and infrastructure.

extra_fields.insight: Understanding climate change feedback mechanisms is essential for predicting the future of our planet and designing effective mitigation strategies.
